section_name: Driving to Left of Center of Roadway: Limitations Other than Passing
text:
(a) An operator may not drive to the left side of the roadway if the operator is:

(1) approaching within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad grade crossing in a municipality;

(2) approaching within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad grade crossing outside a municipality and the intersection or crossing is shown by a sign or marking in accordance with Section 545.055 (Passing to the Left: Passing Zones) ;

(3) approaching within 100 feet of a bridge, viaduct, or tunnel; or

(4) awaiting access to a ferry operated by the Texas Transportation Commission.

(b) The limitations in Subsection (a) do not apply:

(1) on a one-way roadway; or

(2) to an operator turning left into or from an alley or private road or driveway.

(c) The Texas Transportation Commission shall post signs along the approach to a ferry operated by the commission notifying operators that passing is prohibited if there is a standing line of vehicles awaiting access to the ferry.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 545. Operation & Movement of Vehicles
##### § 545.056 — Driving to Left of Center of Roadway: Limitations Other than Passing

(a) An operator may not drive to the left side of the roadway if the operator is:

    (1) approaching within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad grade crossing in a municipality;

    (2) approaching within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad grade crossing outside a municipality and the intersection or crossing is shown by a sign or marking in accordance with Section 545.055 (Passing to the Left: Passing Zones) ;

    (3) approaching within 100 feet of a bridge, viaduct, or tunnel; or

    (4) awaiting access to a ferry operated by the Texas Transportation Commission.

(b) The limitations in Subsection (a) do not apply:

    (1) on a one-way roadway; or

    (2) to an operator turning left into or from an alley or private road or driveway.

(c) The Texas Transportation Commission shall post signs along the approach to a ferry operated by the commission notifying operators that passing is prohibited if there is a standing line of vehicles awaiting access to the ferry.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: An operator may not drive to the left side of the roadway if the operator is:
approaching within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad grade crossing in a municipality;

approaching within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad grade crossing outside a municipality and the intersection or crossing is shown by a sign or marking in accordance with Section 545.055 (Passing to the Left: Passing Zones) ;
approaching within 100 feet of a bridge, viaduct, or tunnel; or
awaiting access to a ferry operated by the Texas Transportation Commission.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The limitations in Subsection (a) do not apply:
to an operator turning left into or from an alley or private road or driveway.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: The Texas Transportation Commission shall post signs along the approach to a ferry operated by the commission notifying operators that passing is prohibited if there is a standing line of vehicles awaiting access to the ferry.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
